findings.evidence: grep: `Quotient.lean:135:lemma orbitQuotientEquivBase_conj ... (f : F) : ... (Quotient.mk'' f ...) = ...`; nearby TauCeti already proves orbit transport under conjugation (`FiberTransport.lean:137` and `:160`), which is the needed well-definedness evidence for the quotient-level map.
findings.fix: Define the quotient map/equivalence induced by an over-base homeomorphism, e.g. `MulAction.orbitRel.Quotient (Deck p) E ≃ MulAction.orbitRel.Quotient (Deck q) F`, and state conjugation compatibility for arbitrary quotient elements; keep the representative lemma as a simp corollary.
findings.issue: `orbitQuotientEquivBase_conj` states compatibility only for `Quotient.mk'' f`, forcing every later use with an arbitrary orbit quotient element to redo quotient induction.

summary: The main quotient/base equivalence is at the right algebraic level, but the conjugation compatibility is exposed only on representatives instead of at the quotient level.
